articles.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
articles.summary: A 20-year-old man was arrested in San Francisco for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's house early Friday morning. He was later apprehended near OpenAI's offices after making threats.

articles.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
articles.summary: Iranian content creators at Explosive Media have produced viral AI-generated Lego videos depicting geopolitical tensions, crediting their success to emotional resonance rather than technical sophistication.

articles.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
articles.summary: Sam Altman's turbulent tenure at OpenAI, including his brief firing and reinstatement, raises questions about his fitness to lead transformative AI technology, as explored in The New Yorker.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.summary: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator following recent Claude pricing changes. The action came after modifications to OpenClaw users' access terms.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.summary: A stalking victim sued OpenAI, claiming ChatGPT ignored three warnings about a dangerous user, including mass-casualty flags, while he harassed his ex-girlfriend.

articles.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
articles.summary: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield heads to Tokyo for SusHi Tech 2026, focusing on AI, robotics, resilience, and entertainment with live demos and discussions on humanoid robots, autonomous driving, cybersecurity, and AI's impact on music and anime.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with two million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure, challenging AWS with simpler alternatives for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200/month for AI coding, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt attracted thousands of developers and successfully hired top engineering talent.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers due to negative customer sentiment regarding Broadcom's VMware takeover strategy.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment centers, and refineries. Six US government agencies issued an urgent warning about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-stealing sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces government funding cuts under the Trump administration due to vaccine skepticism, with RFK Jr. canceling mRNA vaccine projects. The company pivots to rebranding treatments as therapies to navigate regulatory challenges.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: MIT Technology Review features an exclusive sci-fi story by Jeff VanderMeer about crash-landed explorers on a hostile planet discovering alien artifacts, plus coverage of AI models deemed too dangerous to release.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A crash-landed spaceship crew explores a hostile alien planet with a failed rescue beacon. They discover alien artifacts nearby and must venture out to survive.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on consumer GPUs. The system improves visual quality and performance for everyday hardware, making advanced simulations more accessible.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling processing of text, images, and other data types together for improved semantic understanding and ranking tasks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve while performing tasks on the job, rather than requiring pre-training. This approach allows agents to adapt and evolve continuously during deployment.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence and emerging trends in the field, exploring what developments lie ahead for AI technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing campaigns.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit designed to modernize agriculture through AI and technology, enabling farmers to optimize operations and sustainability practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for the Gemini API—Flex and Priority—to help developers balance cost efficiency with latency performance.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technology, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with Brazil's government to create a satellite imagery map designed to monitor and protect the country's forests from deforestation and environmental damage.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more natural, fluid audio interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI behaviors.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and claimed efficiency gains, while Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family. The episode discusses these releases and other major AI developments from the week.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances real-time photorealism up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate new details beyond traditional upscaling.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI news including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub code review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math/science visualization.

articles.title: IBM: How robust AI governance protects enterprise margins
articles.summary: IBM emphasizes that robust AI governance is essential for enterprises to protect margins and securely manage AI infrastructure. As AI evolves from product to foundational infrastructure, governance models must adapt accordingly.

articles.title: Why companies like Apple are building AI agents with limits
articles.summary: Apple and Qualcomm are developing AI agents with built-in safety limits, requiring user approval for sensitive actions like payments. These systems use a human-in-the-loop approach to prevent unauthorized transactions.

articles.title: Meta has a competitive AI model but loses its open-source identity
articles.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark, a proprietary AI model that outperforms Llama 4, marking a shift away from open-source AI despite Llama's billion downloads.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging and system reliability.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture that improves autonomous robot navigation by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems built around them emerge as the true competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that the harness engineering, not the base model itself, forms the real moat.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running OpenClaw AI agents on Mac Minis. The threat targets the growing trend of local AI agent deployment.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a major limitation in AI models.

articles.title: How Knowledge Distillation Compresses Ensemble Intelligence into a Single Deployable AI Model
articles.summary: Knowledge distillation compresses ensemble model intelligence into a single deployable student model using soft probability outputs, maintaining performance while reducing latency and operational complexity.

articles.title: Alibaba’s Tongyi Lab Releases VimRAG: a Multimodal RAG Framework that Uses a Memory Graph to Navigate Massive Visual Contexts
articles.summary: Alibaba's Tongyi Lab introduced VimRAG, a multimodal RAG framework using memory graphs to efficiently handle visual data in large language models, overcoming limitations of traditional text-based RAG approaches.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Markerless 3D Human Kinematics with Pose2Sim, RTMPose, and OpenSim
articles.summary: A tutorial on using Pose2Sim pipeline to perform markerless 3D human kinematics analysis from multi-camera videos, covering calibration, pose estimation, triangulation, and OpenSim integration for biomechanical motion data.

articles.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
articles.summary: The Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver is now 50% off. It's designed for home repairs, DIY projects, and crafts.

articles.title: This Ryobi yard essentials bundle packs a free power tool - how to get yours
articles.summary: Ryobi offers a yard essentials bundle featuring a string trimmer and leaf blower at Home Depot, with a free power tool of your choice included in the sale.

articles.title: 'Finally, a boomless headset for the office': How Jabra made headphones I'd take to a boardroom
articles.summary: Jabra's redesigned Evolve3 75 headset offers a professional design suitable for business settings and hybrid work environments, eliminating the traditional boom microphone for a more polished appearance.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: A three-tiered graph-based RAG system that goes beyond vector search, offering deterministic retrieval through structured graph approaches for more reliable and explainable AI responses.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: The article discusses design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems, providing guidance on implementing autonomous AI agents with structured approaches and best practices.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide for testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. Covers hands-on methods for evaluating agent performance and reliability in production environments.

articles.title: When Things Get Weird with Custom Calendars in Tabular Models
articles.summary: Power BI and Fabric Tabular models introduced Calendar-based Time Intelligence in September 2025, offering powerful capabilities but with potential pitfalls to navigate.

articles.title: Why MLOps Retraining Schedules Fail — Models Don’t Forget, They Get Shocked
articles.summary: Calendar-based ML model retraining fails because models don't gradually forget—they experience sudden performance shocks. A shock-detection approach offers practical solutions for real production systems.

articles.title: A Guide to Voice Cloning on Voxtral with a Missing Encoder
articles.summary: The article explores voice cloning techniques for Voxtral text-to-speech by reconstructing audio codes without a complete encoder. It demonstrates methods to extract voice characteristics from audio samples.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing how robots transform industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster transition from virtual training to real-world deployment.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a game about grit and faith from Liquid Swords, is now available on GeForce NOW cloud streaming service for nearly any device.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Understanding model lifecycle is essential for planning AI applications and testing compatibility before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, helps enterprises discover, share, and reuse agents across organizations while addressing visibility, control, and governance challenges at scale.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView, a React component enabling real-time visibility of AI agent browsing actions. Users can now monitor autonomous web interactions directly within applications for enhanced trust and control.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems, enables large-scale GPU workloads on Kubernetes while preserving existing job scheduling investments and workflows.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP reduces LLM checkpoint storage costs using just 30 lines of Python code. The method compresses massive model snapshots, significantly lowering storage expenses during large-scale training.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: The article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome scale, focusing on how proteins function through interactions forming complexes rather than in isolation, representing quaternary protein structures.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive social media advertising across various platforms, exploring generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these innovations openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology. Their non-traditional path provided unique perspectives that advanced XR development.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models, improving interpretability through feature attribution analysis to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. The approach quantifies imaging system performance through information estimation from noisy measurements.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer approach instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks.
